learning_outcomes.description: Identify the normal reproductive anatomy (structure) and physiology (function), before and after puberty, of the male and female in domestic and exotic animal species and be able to discern how disruptions in structure and function can lead to problems of clinical significance.
Explain the oestrus cycle and its hormonal control and describe how it can be evaluated and manipulated in domestic animals at both the individual and group level to optimise fertility.

Describe the events leading to the initiation (fertilisation) and successful maintenance of pregnancy and its diagnosis in domestic species, detailing the development of the placenta, embryo and foetus, maintenance of gestation, the importance of maternal recognition of pregnancy, and parturition and lactation.
Work together in small groups to read and critically analyse the scientific literature.

assessments.due_date:
Check in meeting (group times will be confirmed during semester) 24/09/2026 1:00 pm

Video due 16/10/2026 2:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
In groups of 4 students, select one manuscript from the available list for critical evaluation. This assignment has 2 parts:

  1. A 5 minute, in person ' check in’ meeting with the course coordinator (10% weighting)
  2. A short (10 minute max) video, including references (10% weighting)


The check in meeting is designed to test your knowledge of the study, its strengths and limitations, and how it fits into the literature. It’s also a chance to reflect on how you’ve worked as a group. The video is aimed at explaining the significance and quality of the manuscript to your peers (e.g. How reliable are the results and conclusions of the paper? To what extent did the paper add to current knowledge at the time of publication?). As part of your evaluation, concisely explain the background reproductive physiology concepts and application of the findings to veterinary practice.

A list of manuscripts, groups and further assessment details will be provided during semester. Guidelines and marking criteria for this assignment will be in the Blackboard assessment folder. 

On the video submission due date, students must submit the assignment coversheet detailing their use of AI, and a reference list (either in the video or as a separate document).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Instructions for submission will be on VETS2008 Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Sequence: Quizzes (Male, female and pregnancy/parturition/lactation)
assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1: Male reproduction 20/08/2026 1:00 pm

Quiz 2: Female reproduction 10/09/2026 1:00 pm

Quiz 3: Pregnancy/parturition/lactation 29/10/2026 1:00 pm

Quizzes will be held during scheduled class time
assessments.task_description:
There will be an assessment sequence consisting of 3 quizzes throughout the semester:

  1. In Week 4: Male reproduction - covers anatomy and physiology of males
  2. In Week 7: Female reproduction - covers anatomy and physiology of females
  3. In Week 13: Pregnancy, parturition and lactation - covers anatomy and physiology of pregnancy, parturition and lactation

Dates are subject to change in consultation with the year group (e.g. to avoid conflict with other major assessments).

All quizzes are online (Blackboard), invigilated in-class quizzes. Each quiz is worth 8%, with a total of 30 questions worth 1 mark each and a time limit of 40 minutes. Students will be given a random selection of questions in random order.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All quizzes are online (Blackboard) and will be conducted as invigilated in-class quizzes.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: The end of semester exam is an on-campus invigilated examination. The exam contributes 56% of the final grade and is a 120 minute exam held during the end of semester exam period. This exam will cover ALL material presented in the lectures, tutorials and practical classes, with a focus on applying knowledge to clinically relevant scenarios. The exam will incorporate a range of question types available in the Inspera examination platform.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
